name: Partridge Under Oil with Asparagus Salad
recipeIngredient: 3 Cups extra virgin olive oil, plus 4 Tbsp
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 6 quarts water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
2. Place partridge in the boiling water and cook gently until tender, about 45 minutes.
3. Carefully remove the partridge and allow it to cool.
4. Remove the legs from the carcass and dry well.
5. Place the legs in a shallow baking dish.
6. Remove the breasts from the carcass with the first wing joint still attached.
7. Dry the breasts well with a towel and place them in a dish with the legs.
8. Make stock out of the bones and cooking liquid.
9. Pour vinegar over the breast and leg pieces, add fennel seeds and garlic.
10. Place in refrigerator for 1 hour.
11. Remove the breast and leg pieces from the refrigerator; remove them from the vinegar.
12. Pat dry and arrange loosely in a large clear cookie jar.
13. Cover with olive oil and refrigerate for 2 days (they will stay preserved this way for up to 2 months).
14. To serve, remove 4 breasts and 4 legs and warm to room temperature.
15. Blanch and refresh the asparagus; toss with 4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil and juice and zest of lemon.
16. Season with salt and pepper and serve at room temperature.
Yield: 4 servings
